title: crataegus × eriocalyx var. villosa
intro: Crataegus × eriocalyx var. villosa, also known as hawthorn, is a deciduous shrub or small tree that is a hybrid of the Crataegus monogyna and Crataegus laevigata species. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and cardiovascular protective effects. The health benefits of hawthorn include reducing blood pressure, improving cardiovascular health, and acting as an antidiabetic agent. Hawthorn is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or capsule, and is commonly used in herbal medicine to promote overall cardiovascular well-being and to support the health of the heart and blood vessels.
